growth_records.lvlwhy: species-level trait compilation (Madin) — no strain recorded in source
growth_records.cite: Madin JS et al. A synthesis of bacterial and archaeal phenotypic trait data. Sci Data 7:170 (2020); source dataset: patric.
growth_records.notes: Species-level trait record; culture mode/pH often unspecified; medium + uptake/secretion pending literature curation.

growth_records.medium: Defined marine anaerobic medium (BS-type) with pyruvate as carbon source
growth_records.cite: Subhash Yadav et al. Organic matter degradation in the deep, sulfidic waters of the Black Sea: insights into the ecophysiology of novel anaerobic bacteria. Microbiome (2024). DOI:10.1186/s40168-024-01816-x. [PMC11129491]
growth_records.snippet: Lutibacter sp. strains B1(T) and B2. Both strains grew faster on pyruvate (doubling time 1.5-2 h; Fig. S19) than on glucose (doubling time 2-4 h; Fig. S19) or cellobiose (doubling time 3-6 h; Fig. S19)
growth_records.notes: Pyruvate doubling time 1.5-2 h; midpoint 1.75 h; mu = 0.693/1.75 = 0.396 h-1. Strain B2 reported jointly.

growth_records.medium: Defined marine anaerobic medium (BS-type) with glucose as carbon source
growth_records.cite: Subhash Yadav et al. Organic matter degradation in the deep, sulfidic waters of the Black Sea: insights into the ecophysiology of novel anaerobic bacteria. Microbiome (2024). DOI:10.1186/s40168-024-01816-x. [PMC11129491]
growth_records.snippet: grew faster on pyruvate (doubling time 1.5-2 h) than on glucose (doubling time 2-4 h; Fig. S19) or cellobiose (doubling time 3-6 h)
growth_records.notes: Glucose doubling time 2-4 h; midpoint 3 h; mu = 0.693/3 = 0.231 h-1 (range 0.173-0.347).

growth_records.medium: Defined marine anaerobic medium (BS-type) with cellobiose as carbon source
growth_records.cite: Subhash Yadav et al. Organic matter degradation in the deep, sulfidic waters of the Black Sea: insights into the ecophysiology of novel anaerobic bacteria. Microbiome (2024). DOI:10.1186/s40168-024-01816-x. [PMC11129491]
growth_records.snippet: than on glucose (doubling time 2-4 h) or cellobiose (doubling time 3-6 h; Fig. S19)
growth_records.notes: Cellobiose doubling time 3-6 h; midpoint 4.5 h; mu = 0.693/4.5 = 0.154 h-1 (range 0.116-0.231).
